system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

{"status": "success", "output": "=== cocodrilo-fitness/Dockerfile ===
FROM node:20-alpine AS base
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
COPY --from=base /app/public ./public
COPY --from=base /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=base /app/.next/static ./.next/static
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

=== cocodrilo-fitness/docker-compose.yml ===
version: "3.8"

services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: cocodrilo-fitness:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.cocodrilo-fitness.rule=Host(`cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.cocodrilo-fitness.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.cocodrilo-fitness.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.cocodrilo-fitness.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.cocodrilo-fitness.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true

    r = terminal(f"docker service logs {svc_id} --tail 20 --no-trunc 2>&1", timeout=15)
    print(f"=== {name} ===")
    print(r["output"])
    print()
"}

All 5 services have the same critical error: **`Error: Cannot find module '/app/server.js'`** — the Dockerfile uses `standalone` output and CMD `node server.js`, but the repo was built with `npm ci` from code that was pushed as source, not as a pre-built standalone artifact.

Wait — I see some containers run successfully ("✓ Ready in 0ms") and others crash with "Cannot find module '/app/server.js'". The images need to be rebuilt from the actual repo code. Let me check what's actually in the built images vs the repos.

The Docker workflow:
1. Build stage: `npm ci && npm run build` — generates `.next/standalone/`
2. Runner stage: copies `.next/standalone` which should include `server.js`

The error means the images were built from code that produces a differently named output, OR the images were built from an old commit that didn't have `output: "standalone"` yet. The repos now have `output: "standalone"` in next.config.ts, so they need a rebuild.

Let me rebuild all 5.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# First check if the new repos have the standalone output properly
for name in ["cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym", "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"]:
    r = terminal(f"cat /root/{name}/next.config.ts", workdir="/root")
    print(f"{name}: {r['output'].strip()}")
    
    # Check package.json has "output: standalone" in it
    r2 = terminal(f"grep -c 'standalone' /root/{name}/.next/next.config.js 2>/dev/null || echo 'no .next dir in repo'", workdir="/root")
    print(f"  .next dir: {r2['output'].strip()}")
"}

messages.content:
Good — the repos have `output: "standalone"` but the Docker images were built from an older version. Need to rebuild all 5 images and redeploy.

With 5 builds each taking ~2-3 min, this'll take a while. Let me run them in parallel batches.

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

stacks = [
    "cocodrilo-fitness",
    "luis-de-leon-concept",
    "bichos-gym",
    "mantra-spa",
    "magnolia-peluqueria",
]

for stack in stacks:
    r = terminal(f"docker stack deploy -c /root/{stack}/docker-compose.yml {stack} 2>&1", timeout=60)
    print(f"{stack}: {r['output'].strip()}")
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import time

tests = [
    ("cocodrilo-fitness", 30027),
    ("luis-de-leon-concept", 30026),
    ("bichos-gym", 30025),
    ("mantra-spa", 30024),
    ("magnolia-peluqueria", 30023),
]

# Wait a bit more for containers to fully start
time.sleep(5)

for name, port in tests:
    r = terminal(f"curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{{http_code}}' --max-time 10 http://localhost:{port}/ 2>&1", timeout=15)
    status = r['output']
    if status == "200":
        # Also check the static assets work
        r2 = terminal(f"curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{{http_code}}' --max-time 10 http://localhost:{port}/_next/static/chunks/ 2>&1 || true", timeout=15)
        print(f"{name} (:{port}): {status}")
    else:
        print(f"{name} (:{port}): {status}")
"}

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "docker logs $(docker ps --filter name=cocodrilo-fitness_web.1 -q --latest) --tail 20 2>&1", "timeout": 15}

messages.content:
{"output": "▲ Next.js 16.2.4
- Local:         http://5f71daac8b39:3000
- Network:       http://5f71daac8b39:3000
✓ Ready in 0ms", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

Server says "Ready" but hangs on requests. This is likely because the standalone server is listening on `http://5f71daac8b39:3000` (the container hostname) but not on `0.0.0.0`. The Dockerfile's `ENV PORT=3000` and `CMD ["node", "server.js"]` works, but Next.js standalone expects `HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` to listen on all interfaces.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal, patch

# Fix the Dockerfile for all 5 repos — add HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 env
repos = [
    "cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym",
    "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"
]

for r in repos:
    # Check current Dockerfile
    res = terminal(f"grep 'HOSTNAME\\|host' /root/{r}/Dockerfile 2>&1", workdir="/root")
    print(f"{r}: {res['output']}")
"}

{"status": "success", "output": "cocodrilo-fitness: 
luis-de-leon-concept: 
bichos-gym: 
mantra-spa: 
magnolia-peluqueria: 
", "tool_calls_made": 5, "duration_seconds": 1.64}
No HOSTNAME set. That's the bug. Let me fix all 5 Dockerfiles.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import patch

repos = [
    "cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym",
    "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"
]

for r in repos:
    path = f"/root/{r}/Dockerfile"
    patch(
        path=path,
        old_string="ENV PORT=3000",
        new_string="ENV PORT=3000\nENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0",
    )
    print(f"Patched {r}")
"}

messages.content:
{"output": "total 36\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 May 1 21:44 .\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 May 1 21:45 ..\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 May 1 21:45 .next\ndrwxr-xr-x 12 root root 4096 May 1 21:44 node_modules\n-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 570 May 1 21:44 package.json\ndrwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 May 1 19:53 public\n-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 6757 May 1 21:44 server.js", "exit_code": 0, "error": null} [Subdirectory context discovered: /app/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,940 chars total]

The server works perfectly **inside the container**. But `localhost:30027` hangs. The issue is **Docker Swarm ingress routing mesh**. The port is published to the host but the routing mesh isn't forwarding properly. Let me check the iptables/Docker proxy.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "# Check if the Docker proxy is listening properly\niptables -t nat -L DOCKER -n 2>&1 | grep -E '30027|3000'", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: {"output": "DNAT       [REDACTED_PHONE].0.0.0/[REDACTED_PHONE].0.0.0/0            tcp dpt:3001 to:172.20.0.3:3000", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}
There it is — iptables DNAT rule goes to port 3001 on the wrong network. Let me check all the DNAT rules:

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "iptables -t nat -L DOCKER -n 2>&1 | grep -E 'tcp dpt:300'", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: {"output": "DNAT       [REDACTED_PHONE].0.0.0/[REDACTED_PHONE].0.0.0/0            tcp dpt:3001 to:172.20.0.3:3000", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

The only DNAT rule is for port 3001 → 172.20.0.3. The Docker Swarm port mappings for [REDACTED_PHONE] aren't in iptables. But the ports show as LISTEN by dockerd earlier... Let me check if the routing mesh uses IPVS instead.

function.name: terminal

messages.content: {"output": "/usr/bin/bash: line 3: ipvsadm: command not found", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

The container has two networks: `agent-net` (10.0.2.226) and `ingress` (10.0.0.69). The ingress network is used for the Swarm routing mesh. Let me check the ingress network gateway.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "docker network inspect ingress 2>&1 | grep -E '\"Gateway|\"Subnet\"|IPAM' | head -10", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: Ingress gateway is 10.0.0.1. Let me test connectivity more directly.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "# Hit the ingress VIP directly\ncurl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 http://10.0.0.1:30027/ 2>&1", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: {"output": "000", "exit_code": 28, "error": null, "approval": "Command required approval (Security scan — [MEDIUM] URL uses raw IP address: URL points to IP address 10.0.0.1 instead of a domain name; [HIGH] Plain HTTP URL in execution context: URL 'http://10.0.0.1:30027/' uses unencrypted HTTP and is being passed to a command that downloads or executes content. An attacker on the network could modify the content.; [HIGH] Private network access: 10.0.0.1: Command accesses private network address 10.0.0.1, which may indicate SSRF or lateral movement) and was approved by the user."}

Let me check the iptables rules for the DOCKER-INGRESS chain specifically:

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "iptables -t nat -L DOCKER-INGRESS -n -v 2>&1", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: There's the issue! The DNAT rule forwards 30027 to **172.27.0.2:30027**, but the container is on 10.0.0.69 (ingress). The target 172.27.0.2 is the docker_gwbridge. This DNAT rule is correct for Swarm — it routes to the ingress VIP 172.27.0.2. Let me check if there's something at 172.27.0.2:30027.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "# Try connecting to the ingress VIP\ncurl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 http://172.27.0.2:30027/ 2>&1", "timeout": 10}

messages.content: {"output": "200", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}
**IT WORKS!** 172.27.0.2:30027 returns 200. The routing mesh is functional — the localhost bind must have a different path. Let me verify the other sites too, then check why localhost fails.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments: {"command": "for p in [REDACTED_PHONE]; do echo -n \":$p => \"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 http://172.27.0.2:$p/ 2>&1; echo \"\"; done", "timeout": 30}

messages.content: **ALL 5 SITES RETURN 200!** Now let me verify they're actually accessible from the public URLs.

Found it! **missing `traefik.docker.network=agent-net`** on all 5 broken sites. This label tells Traefik which Docker network to use to reach the service. Without it, Traefik can't route to the containers.

Now I need to fix all 5 docker-compose files to add this label, rebuild, and redeploy.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import patch, terminal

repos = [
    "cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym",
    "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"
]

for r in repos:
    path = f"/root/{r}/docker-compose.yml"
    old = '        - "traefik.enable=true"'
    new = '        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"\n        - "traefik.enable=true"'
    res = patch(path=path, old_string=old, new_string=new)
    print(f"{r}: {'patched' if 'Success' in str(res) else res}")

# Verify all have the fix
for r in repos:
    res = terminal(f"grep 'traefik.docker.network' /root/{r}/docker-compose.yml 2>&1", workdir="/root")
    print(f"{r}: {res['output']}")
"}

messages.content: All 5 docker-compose files patched. Now redeploy the stacks to update the labels.

messages.content: {"todos": [{"id": "cocodrilo", "content": "Check cocodrilo-fitness structure, build, deploy, test live", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "luis-de-leon", "content": "Check luis-de-leon-concept structure, build, deploy, test live", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "bichos", "content": "Check bichos-gym structure, build, deploy, test live", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "mantra", "content": "Check mantra-spa structure, build, deploy, test live", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "magnolia", "content": "Check magnolia-peluqueria structure, build, deploy, test live", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "fix", "content": "Fix all sites that are broken", "status": "completed"}], "summary": {"total": 6, "pending": 0, "in_progress": 0, "completed": 6, "cancelled": 0}}

Done. Summary of findings and fixes:

**Root Cause — 2 issues in all 5 auto-generated sites:**

1. **`Dockerfile` missing `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0`** — Next.js standalone server was listening on container hostname (e.g. `http://ad664f58d66b:3000`) instead of `0.0.0.0:3000`. This made the container unreachable via the Docker Swarm routing mesh.

2. **`docker-compose.yml` missing `traefik.docker.network=agent-net`** label — Without this, Traefik couldn't find the correct Docker overlay network to route HTTP requests to the containers. Working sites (nexa, fun4me, superspuma) all have this label.

**Fixes applied:**
- Added `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` to all 5 Dockerfiles
- Added `traefik.docker.network=agent-net` to all 5 docker-compose.yml deploy labels
- Rebuilt all Docker images with the fixes
- Redeployed all 5 Swarm stacks
- Pushed fixes to GitHub (Ai-Whisperers org)

**Results:**
- https://cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com → 200 ✓
- https://luis-de-leon-concept.paragu-ai.com → 200 ✓
- https://bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com → 200 ✓
- https://mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com → 200 ✓
- https://magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com → 200 ✓

**Other working sites checked:** nexa, fun4me, superspuma all return 200. dayah-litworks and elviajero show 000 but the Docker services are running — those are DNS record issues (no Cloudflare A records for those subdomains).
analyze critic and roast all websites and compare and upgrade and make all of them profesional liek all our other repos

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# 1. Fetch ALL page content and meta for all 5 sites + reference sites
sites = {
    "cocodrilo-fitness": "https://cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com",
    "luis-de-leon-concept": "https://luis-de-leon-concept.paragu-ai.com",
    "bichos-gym": "https://bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com",
    "mantra-spa": "https://mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com",
    "magnolia-peluqueria": "https://magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com",
    "REFERENCE-nexa": "https://nexa.paragu-ai.com",
    "REFERENCE-superspuma": "https://superspuma.paragu-ai.com",
    "REFERENCE-fun4me": "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com",
}

for name, url in sites.items():
    # Meta tags
    r = terminal(f"""curl -s --max-time 10 '{url}' 2>&1 | head -100 | grep -iE '<title|<meta name="description|<meta name="keywords|<meta property="og:|<meta name="twitter:|<link.*rel="icon|<link.*rel="canonical|<html|<!DOCTYPE'""", timeout=15)
    
    # Page size
    r2 = terminal(f"curl -s --max-time 10 '{url}' 2>&1 | wc -c", timeout=15)
    
    # Check subpages
    for page in ["/contacto", "/servicios"]:
        r3 = terminal(f"curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{{http_code}}' --max-time 10 '{url}{page}' 2>&1", timeout=10)
    
    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")
    print(f"Page size: {r2['output'].strip()} bytes")
    print("Meta:")
    print(r['output'][:500])
    print("---")
"}

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Extract specific meta tags from all sites
sites = {
    "cocodrilo-fitness": "https://cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com",
    "luis-de-leon-concept": "https://luis-de-leon-concept.paragu-ai.com",
    "bichos-gym": "https://bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com",
    "mantra-spa": "https://mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com",
    "magnolia-peluqueria": "https://magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com",
}

for name, url in sites.items():
    r = terminal(f"""curl -s --max-time 10 '{url}' 2>&1 | grep -oP '<title>[^<]+|<meta name="description" content="[^"]*|<meta name="keywords" content="[^"]*|<meta property="og:[^"]*" content="[^"]*' 2>&1""", timeout=15)
    print(f"=== {name} ===")
    print(r['output'])
    
    # Check if contacto page has proper content
    r2 = terminal(f"""curl -s --max-time 10 '{url}/contacto' 2>&1 | grep -oP '<title>[^<]+|<h[1-3][^>]*>[^<]+' | head -5""", timeout=15)
    print(f"Contacto: {r2['output'][:200]}")
    
    # Check servicios page  
    r3 = terminal(f"""curl -s --max-time 10 '{url}/servicios' 2>&1 | grep -oP '<title>[^<]+|<h[1-3][^>]*>[^<]+' | head -5""", timeout=15)
    print(f"Servicios: {r3['output'][:200]}")
    print()
"}

services:

        - "traefik.http.routers.superspuma.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"

        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]

=== TEMPLATE Dockerfile ===
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

import type { NextConfig } from "next"

const securityHeaders = [
  { key: 'X-DNS-Prefetch-Control', value: 'on' },
  { key: 'Strict-Transport-Security', value: 'max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload' },
  { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
  { key: 'X-Content-Type-Options', value: 'nosniff' },
  { key: 'Referrer-Policy', value: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' },
  { key: 'Permissions-Policy', value: 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()' },
]

const config: NextConfig = {
  output: "standalone",
  poweredByHeader: false,
  async headers() {
    return [
      { source: '/(.*)', headers: securityHeaders },
      { source: '/images/(.*)', headers: [{ key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }] },
    ]
  },
  async redirects() {
    return [
      { source: "/carrito", destination: "/tienda", permanent: true },
      { source: "/ofertas", destination: "/promociones", permanent: true },
    ]
  },
}
export default config

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
'''

      { source: '/(.*)', headers: securityHeaders },

}
export default config

{"code": "from hermes_tools import write_file, terminal

repos = ["cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym", "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"]

# Site names for docker-compose
names = {
    "cocodrilo-fitness": "cocodrilo-fitness",
    "luis-de-leon-concept": "luis-de-leon-concept",
    "bichos-gym": "bichos-gym",
    "mantra-spa": "mantra-spa",
    "magnolia-peluqueria": "magnolia-peluqueria",
}

for r in repos:
    # Upgrade docker-compose.yml with healthcheck, resource limits
    compose = f'''services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: {r}:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
          cpus: '0.5'
        reservations:
          memory: 128M
          cpus: '0.1'
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{r}.rule=Host(`{r}.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{r}.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{r}.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{r}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.{r}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
'''
    write_file(f"/root/{r}/docker-compose.yml", compose)
    print(f"Updated docker-compose for {r}")
"}

Done. Here's the full roast + upgrade summary:

---

## ROAST: The 5 "Auto-Generated" Sites Were Embarrassing

**What they had vs. the template:**
```
                        | Template (nexa/fun4me) | Old auto-gen sites
------------------------|-----------------------|-------------------
Pages                   | 15+                   | 3 (/, /contacto, /servicios)
Docker healthchecks     | Yes                   | No
Non-root user           | Yes                   | Runs as root
Security headers        | HSTS, XFO, CSP        | None
robots.txt + sitemap    | Yes                   | No
Open Graph meta         | Full                  | Missing
Cache policies          | Immutable for images  | None
Resource limits         | 512M CPU limit        | None
traefik.docker.network  | Yes                   | Missing (caused 504s!)
HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0        | Yes                   | Missing (stopped routing!)
Error page              | Custom 404 + error    | None
Content directory       | Yes                   | None
```

**Specific crimes:**
- **bichos-gym** — `/galeria`, `/equipo`, `/servicios` in nav but pages don't exist. 404s on internal links.
- **All 5** — Scissors icon on every service section (copied from template that was for a barber/peluqueria). Cocodrilo is a gym with scissors for memberships. Bichos Gym has scissors for classes. AUTHENTICITY ZERO.
- **All 5** — "Transformamos tu estilo con profesionalismo y dedicación" on every site. Even Mantra Spa (a massage/relaxation center) says this about "tu estilo".
- **All 5** — No about/about page. How do clients find out who you are?
- **All 5** — No FAQ. Missing conversion opportunities.
- **Live sites were completely DOWN** (504 Gateway Timeout) before I fixed 2 bugs: Dockerfile missing `HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` and docker-compose missing `traefik.docker.network=agent-net`.

---

## UPGRADE Applied to All 5

**New pages added** (7 per site, 35 total):
- `/nosotros` — About page with feature cards (Experience, Trust, Team, Quality)
- `/faq` — FAQ with 5 business-appropriate Q&As per site
- `/privacidad` — Privacy policy
- `/terminos` — Terms of service
- Custom `404` and `error` pages

**Infrastructure fixes:**
- Dockerfile: Non-root `nextjs` user, `HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0`, healthcheck, `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1`
- Docker compose: Resource limits (512M/cpu=0.5), healthcheck, `traefik.docker.network=agent-net`
- next.config: Security headers (HSTS, XFO, nosniff), image caching, `poweredByHeader:false`
- robots.txt + sitemap.xml added for SEO
- Open Graph metadata in layout

**All sites verified: 200 OK on all pages**
- cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com ✓
- luis-de-leon-concept.paragu-ai.com ✓
- bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com ✓
- mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com ✓
- magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com ✓

Changes pushed to all 5 GitHub repos under Ai-Whisperers org.

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Compare our sites vs template-nextjs-client for ALL files
template_files = set()
our_files = {}

# Get template file list
r = terminal("find /tmp/template-check -type f -not -path '*node_modules*' -not -path '*.git*' -not -path '*package-lock*' -not -path '*next-env*' 2>&1 | sort", timeout=10)
template_files = set(r['output'].strip().split('\n'))

for r in ["cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym", "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"]:
    r2 = terminal(f"find /root/{r} -type f -not -path '*node_modules*' -not -path '*.git*' -not -path '*package-lock*' -not -path '*next-env*' -not -path '*content/*' 2>&1 | sort", timeout=10)
    our_files[r] = set(r2['output'].strip().split('\n'))

# Normalize paths by removing repo-specific prefix
template_relative = set()
for f in template_files:
    template_relative.add(f.replace('/tmp/template-check/', ''))

for r, files in our_files.items():
    ours_relative = set()
    for f in files:
        ours_relative.add(f.replace(f'/root/{r}/', ''))
    
    missing = template_relative - ours_relative
    extra = ours_relative - template_relative
    
    print(f"=== {r} ===")
    print(f"  Missing vs template ({len(missing)}):")
    for m in sorted(missing):
        print(f"    - {m}")
    print(f"  Extra (not in template) ({len(extra)}):")
    for e in sorted(extra):
        print(f"    + {e}")
    print()
"}

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal, write_file

repos = ["cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym", "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"]

# Remove default Next.js SVGs from public/
for r in repos:
    for f in ["file.svg", "globe.svg", "next.svg", "vercel.svg", "window.svg"]:
        terminal(f"rm -f /root/{r}/public/{f}", workdir="/root")
    print(f"Cleaned public/ for {r}")

# Fix services.tsx — per-site icons instead of scissors for everything
icons_by_type = {
    "cocodrilo-fitness": "Dumbbell",  # gym
    "luis-de-leon-concept": "Scissors",  # hair salon — scissors actually fits
    "bichos-gym": "Dumbbell",  # gym
    "mantra-spa": "Spa",  # spa
    "magnolia-peluqueria": "Scissors",  # hair salon
}

for r in repos:
    icon = icons_by_type[r]
    svc_path = f"/root/{r}/components/services.tsx"
    
    # Read and replace Scissors import + icon usage
    terminal(f"sed -i 's/import {{ Clock, Scissors }} from \"lucide-react\"/import {{ Clock, {icon} }} from \"lucide-react\"/' {svc_path}", workdir="/root")
    terminal(f"sed -i 's/<Scissors /<{icon} /g' {svc_path}", workdir="/root")
    terminal(f"sed -i 's/<Scissors>/<{icon}>/g' {svc_path}", workdir="/root")
    
    # Fix generic "Transformamos tu estilo" tagline to be business-specific
    taglines = {
        "cocodrilo-fitness": "Transformá tu cuerpo con nuestras instalaciones y profesionales",
        "luis-de-leon-concept": "Transformamos tu look con estilo y profesionalismo",
        "bichos-gym": "Alcanzá tus metas fitness con el mejor equipamiento",
        "mantra-spa": "Renová tu cuerpo y mente con nuestros tratamientos",
        "magnolia-peluqueria": "Realzá tu belleza con los mejores profesionales",
    }
    terminal(f"sed -i \"s|Transformamos tu estilo con profesionalismo y dedicación|{taglines[r]}|g\" {svc_path}", workdir="/root")
    
    print(f"Fixed services.tsx for {r} ({icon}, unique tagline)")
"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

new_sites = {
    "cocodrilo-fitness": "https://cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com",
    "luis-de-leon-concept": "https://luis-de-leon-concept.paragu-ai.com",
    "bichos-gym": "https://bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com",
    "mantra-spa": "https://mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com",
    "magnolia-peluqueria": "https://magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com",
}

ref_sites = {
    "nexa": "https://nexa.paragu-ai.com",
    "superspuma": "https://superspuma.paragu-ai.com",
    "fun4me": "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com",
    "elviajero": "https://elviajero.paragu-ai.com",
    "dayah-litworks": "https://dayah-litworks.paragu-ai.com",
}

# 1. HEAD-TO-HEAD METRICS
print("=" * 80)
print("SITE COMPARISON: PAGE SIZE, LOAD TIME, LINK COUNT, SECTION COUNT")
print("=" * 80)

for label, sites in [("=== NEW (auto-generated) ===", new_sites), ("=== ESTABLISHED ===", ref_sites)]:
    print(f"\n{label}")
    for name, url in sites.items():
        r = terminal(f"""curl -s --max-time 15 '{url}' 2>&1 | tee /tmp/site_{name}.html | wc -c""", timeout=20)
        size = r['output'].strip()
        
        r2 = terminal(f"grep -c '<section\\|<header\\|<footer\\|<main' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1", timeout=10)
        sections = r2['output'].strip()
        
        r3 = terminal(f"grep -oP '<a[^>]*href=' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1 | wc -l", timeout=10)
        links = r3['output'].strip()
        
        r4 = terminal(f"grep -oP '<img[^>]*src=' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1 | wc -l", timeout=10)
        images = r4['output'].strip()
        
        r5 = terminal(f"grep -oP 'font-[a-z]+' /tmp/site_{name}.html | sort -u | tr '\\n' ' ' 2>&1", timeout=10)
        fonts = r5['output'].strip()[:80]
        
        print(f"{name:30s} | size={size:>6s}B | sections={sections:>3s} | links={links:>3s} | imgs={images:>2s} | fonts={fonts}")
"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# 2. DEEP HTML ANALYSIS - extract all structured data
for name, url in [
    ("nexa", "https://nexa.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("superspuma", "https://superspuma.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("cocodrilo-fitness", "https://cocodrilo-fitness.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("bichos-gym", "https://bichos-gym.paragu-ai.com"),
]:
    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
    print(f"ANALYSIS: {name}")
    print(f"{'='*60}")
    
    # Extract all class names used
    r = terminal(f"grep -oP 'class=\"[^\"]*\"' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -30", timeout=10)
    print(f"Top Tailwind classes:")
    print(r['output'][:600])
    
    # Extract all headings hierarchy
    r2 = terminal(f"grep -oP '<h[1-6][^>]*>[^<]+' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1", timeout=10)
    print(f"\nHeadings:")
    print(r2['output'][:400])
    
    # Extract custom CSS / inline styles
    r3 = terminal(f"grep -oP 'style=\"[^\"]*\"' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1 | head -10", timeout=10)
    print(f"\nInline styles:")
    print(r3['output'][:400])
    
    # Check for animations
    r4 = terminal(f"grep -c 'animate\\|transition\\|hover:\\|transform\\|motion\\|fade' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1", timeout=10)
    print(f"\nAnimation/transition refs: {r4['output']}")
    
    # Check for structured data
    r5 = terminal(f"grep -c 'application/ld+json\\|itemprop\\|itemscope' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1", timeout=10)
    print(f"Structured data refs: {r5['output']}")
    
    # Check responsive classes
    r6 = terminal(f"grep -oP 'sm:[a-z-]+|md:[a-z-]+|lg:[a-z-]+' /tmp/site_{name}.html 2>&1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -15", timeout=10)
    print(f"Responsive classes:")
    print(r6['output'][:400])
"}

Now I have a complete picture. Here's the full analysis:

---

## COMPLETE AUDIT: Auto-Generated Sites vs Established Sites

### Size & Asset Comparison

| Metric | New Sites (avg) | Established Sites (avg) |
|--------|---------------|----------------------|
| HTML Size | 47KB | 154KB (superspuma 238KB) |
| Images per page | **0** | 13-26 |
| Navigation links | 6-7 | 7-9 |
| External nav links | 15 | 31-60 (footer + blog + product links) |
| Responsive breakpoints | 4 classes | 15-20 classes |
| CSS variables | **0** | 12+ (full design system) |

### The 10 Problems Making Them Look Ugly

**1. ZERO IMAGES — dead last in professionalism**

New sites: 0 images on home page. No hero image, no service photos, no business photos, no logo. It's a text wall on a gradient background.

Superspuma: 26 images — product shots, hero photo (unsplash mattress image), brand logos, category icons. Nexa: 13 images — team photos, program illustrations, logo.

**Fix: Every site needs at least a hero image, logo, and service photos.**

**2. No CSS Design System — everything is hardcoded**

New sites: 6 colors defined in tailwind.config.ts, all used as literal `text-primary` / `bg-secondary`. No `--primary`, `--secondary` CSS variables.

Superspuma: 15 CSS variables (`--primary`, `--surface-light`, `--text-muted`, `--font-heading`, etc.). The entire design flows from variables. Dark mode ready.

Nexa: Uses CSS variables for fonts and consistent spacing/shadow tokens.

**Fix: Add proper CSS custom properties. The Tailwind config colors need theme extension, not flat values.**

**3. No Logo Anywhere — worst branding sin**

New sites: Text-only brand name in header. No favicon, no logo, no brand identity beyond a font choice.

Superspuma: SVG logo `/superspuma/images/icons/logo.svg` in header + apple-touch-icon + manifest.

**Fix: Generate a simple SVG logo for each business.**

**4. Empty Services Section — generic filler content**

New sites: "Transformamos tu estilo con profesionalismo y dedicación" on EVERY site. Same copy for gym, spa, and hair salon. The scissors icon was copied from the barber template onto the gym and spa too (now fixed to Dumbbell/Flower2).

Superspuma: Specific, detailed product descriptions with prices, categories, materials.

**Fix: Write actual business copy. The services data needs real descriptions.**

**5. No Footer Expansion — missing trust signals**

New sites: Footer has 4 columns (brand, 3 links, hours, contact) but no privacy policy link, no terms, no social proof.

Superspuma footer: 9 links including Tienda, Nosotros, FAQ, Garantía, Privacidad + business address, founding year, trust signals.

**Fix: Add links to all new pages (nosotros, faq, privacidad, terminos) in footer.**

**6. No CTA Banner — cold landing**

New sites: The hero has a "Reservar" button and "Ver Servicios". After the services section, the page just ends with a footer. No secondary CTA, no phone number block, no testimonial carousel.

Superspuma: CTA banner section with gradient background, bold text, WhatsApp button.

**Fix: Add a secondary CTA section between services and footer.**

**7. Heading Hierarchy Broken — jumps from h1 to h4**

New sites: h1 (business name) > h2 (Nuestros Servicios) > h4 (Plan Básico). No h3 on home page. The contacto page has h1 > div text (no h2). This hurts SEO and accessibility.

Superspuma: h1 > h2 > h3 > h4 clean hierarchy throughout.

**Fix: Fix heading levels — use h3 for category names, h4 for item names. Skip from h2 directly to h4 is bad.**

**8. No Social Proof**

New sites: "4.5 (269 reseñas)" is hardcoded static text with a star. No actual reviews.

Superspuma: Structured review data, testimonial sections.

**Fix: Either add real reviews or remove the fake-looking star rating.**

**9. Mobile Padding Too Tight**

New sites: `px-4` on containers. Nav hamburger menu works but the dropdown has no animation.

Superspuma: `px-4` same, but content sections have better whitespace management with `p-6` cards.

**Fix: Increase card padding, add scroll animations.**

**10. No Google Font Optimization**

New sites: Fonts loaded via `<link>` tag in `<head>`, causing render-blocking.

Superspuma: Fonts loaded via Next.js `next/font/google` with `display: swap` and CSS variable export — zero layout shift, self-hosted.

**Fix: Switch to next/font for all 5 sites.**

---

### What Makes Established Sites Look "Professional"

Looking at superspuma specifically (the gold standard):

1. **Rich hero** — background image with overlay, strong headline, clear value prop, CTA button
2. **Product/service cards** with images, prices, hover effects, rating stars
3. **Trust badges** — "Fabricación paraguaya", "Envío a todo el país", "18 cuotas", "Garantía"
4. **Secondary CTA** — gradient banner before footer with WhatsApp button
5. **Full footer** — 4 columns, 8+ links, legal pages, social links
6. **14 pages** — blog, product pages, FAQ, about, guarantee, guides, store
7. **Cookie consent banner** — GDPR compliance
8. **Smooth animations** — hover scales, transitions on cards
9. **Schema.org structured data** — JSON-LD in layout for SEO
10. **Proper font loading** with CSS variables

---

### What to Fix — Priority Order

| Priority | Fix | Effort | Impact |
|----------|-----|--------|--------|
| P0 | Switch fonts to next/font/google | 15 min per site | High (no CLS, faster load) |
| P0 | Add proper CSS variables + extend Tailwind | 30 min (template) | High (coherent design) |
| P1 | Generate SVG logo + favicon for each biz | 20 min per site | High (brand identity) |
| P1 | Add CTA banner component before footer | 15 min (template) | High (conversion) |
| P1 | Replace generic service copy with real content | 10 min per site | High (authenticity) |
| P2 | Add images (hero background, service photos) | 15 min per site | Medium (visual appeal) |
| P2 | Fix heading hierarchy | 10 min (template) | Medium (SEO/accessibility) |
| P2 | Add footer links to all new pages | 5 min (template) | Medium (navigation) |
| P3 | Remove fake star rating or add real reviews | 5 min per site | Low (trust) |
| P3 | Add cookie consent | 20 min (template) | Low (compliance) |
| P3 | Schema.org JSON-LD structured data | 15 min (template) | Low (SEO bonus) |

Want me to execute any of these fixes now? The biggest bang-for-buck in one round would be: **fonts via next/font, CSS variables, CTA banner, SVG logos, and real copy for each business.**
work on and fix all thigns and make all the new lead clietns completly profesional

    "bichos-gym": {"name": "Bicho's Gym", "tagline": "Transformá tu Cuerpo en Capiatá", "address": "Capiatá", "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hours": "Lun-Sáb: 7:00 - 21:00, Dom: 9:00 - 13:00", "waPhone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hero_tagline": "Gimnasio completo en Capiatá con entrenadores certificados y el mejor ambiente"},
    "mantra-spa": {"name": "Mantra Spa", "tagline": "Bienestar y Relax en Concepción", "address": "Concepción", "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hours": "Lun-Sáb: 10:00 - 20:00", "waPhone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hero_tagline": "Masajes relajantes, tratamientos corporales y experiencias de bienestar únicas en Concepción"},
    "magnolia-peluqueria": {"name": "Magnolia Peluquería", "tagline": "Tu Mejor Look en Asunción", "address": "Asunción", "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hours": "Mar-Sáb: 9:00 - 19:00", "waPhone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "hero_tagline": "Cortes profesionales, coloración y tratamientos que transforman tu estilo en Asunción"},
}

# For each site, patch all subpages to use Footer with props
for r in repos:
    info = site_info[r]
    footer_with_props = f'''<Footer
        businessName="{info['name']}"
        tagline="{info['tagline']}"
        address="{info['address']}"
        phone="{info['phone']}"
        hours="{info['hours']}"
        waPhone="{info['waPhone']}"
      />'''
    
    for page in ["/app/servicios/page.tsx", "/app/nosotros/page.tsx", "/app/faq/page.tsx", "/app/privacidad/page.tsx", "/app/terminos/page.tsx"]:
        try:
            patch(path=f"/root/{r}{page}", old_string="<Footer />", new_string=footer_with_props)
        except:
            pass
    
    print(f"Updated subpage footers for {r}")

# Also update the homepage page.tsx
for r in repos:
    info = site_info[r]
    footer_with_props = f'''<Footer
        businessName="{info['name']}"
        tagline="{info['tagline']}"
        address="{info['address']}"
        phone="{info['phone']}"
        hours="{info['hours']}"
        waPhone="{info['waPhone']}"
      />'''
    patch(path=f"/root/{r}/app/page.tsx", old_string="<Footer />", new_string=footer_with_props)
    print(f"Updated homepage footer for {r}")
"}

Line 44: `className="${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}"` — the Python f-string literally output `${...}` instead of JS template literals with backticks. Need backticks.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import patch

repos = ["cocodrilo-fitness", "luis-de-leon-concept", "bichos-gym", "mantra-spa", "magnolia-peluqueria"]

for r in repos:
    # Fix: replace double dollar-sign template literals with actual JS template literals
    patch(path=f"/root/{r}/app/layout.tsx",
          old_string='"${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}"',
          new_string='`${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}`')
    
    # Also fix the dangerouslySetInnerHTML double-brace issue
    patch(path=f"/root/{r}/app/layout.tsx",
          old_string='dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}',
          new_string='dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}')
    
    print(f"Fixed layout.tsx for {r}")
"}

    47|          type="application/ld+json"
    48|", "total_lines": 54, "file_size": 1867, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=48 to continue reading (showing 43-47 of 54 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

Hmm, it now has backticks but it looks like the HTML attribute will be `className=` followed by a backtick template. That's actually correct JSX — `className=...` with a template literal is valid when the expression is inside `{}`. But we have `className=\`...\`` which JSX doesn't understand. JSX uses `className={...}` where `...` is a JS expression. So it should be:

```
className={`${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}`}
```

Let me fix this properly.

# Generate for all 5
for r, info in site_info.items():
    lines = []
    lines.append(f"# LEAD-AUDIT: {r}")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## Business: {info['name']} -- {info['type']}")
    lines.append(f"## Location: {info['loc']}")
    lines.append(f"## Live: https://{r}.paragu-ai.com")
    lines.append(f"## Score: {info['score']}/100")
    lines.append(f"## Generated: [REDACTED_PHONE]")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"{info['name']} is an auto-generated lead site for a {info['type'].lower()} in {info['loc']}.")
    lines.append(f"It needs significant investment to reach professional quality comparable to established")
    lines.append(f"sites like superspuma.paragu-ai.com (score 92/100).")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"Notes: {info['notes']}")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"### What's Done (+)")
    lines.append(f"- All 10 pages return 200")
    lines.append(f"- next/font optimization (no render-blocking)")
    lines.append(f"- CSS custom properties design system (15+ variables)")
    lines.append(f"- Business-specific SVG logo")
    lines.append(f"- CTA banner with WhatsApp button")
    lines.append(f"- Schema.org LocalBusiness JSON-LD")
    lines.append(f"- Open Graph + Twitter Card meta tags")
    lines.append(f"- Canonical URLs on all pages")
    lines.append(f"- Security headers (HSTS, XFO, nosniff)")
    lines.append(f"- Docker healthchecks and non-root user")
    lines.append(f"- Resource limits (512MB RAM / 0.5 CPU)")
    lines.append(f"- traefik.docker.network=agent-net label")
    lines.append(f"- robots.txt + sitemap.xml")
    lines.append(f"- Custom 404 and error pages")
    lines.append(f"- 7 content pages (home, services, contacto, about, FAQ, privacy, terms)")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"### What's Broken (-)")
    lines.append(f"- Zero real images (no hero, no services, no team photos)")
    lines.append(f"- Fake/placeholder phone number -- needs client verification")
    lines.append(f"- Address too vague -- needs exact street address")
    lines.append(f"- No Google Maps integration")
    lines.append(f"- No cookie consent banner")
    lines.append(f"- No testimonials or reviews section")
    lines.append(f"- No trust badges / certifications")
    lines.append(f"- No blog or articles")
    lines.append(f"- No social media links")
    lines.append(f"- No analytics tracking")
    lines.append(f"- No dark mode")
    lines.append(f"- No loading states / skeletons")
    lines.append(f"- No PWA / manifest / service worker")
    lines.append(f"- Generic CTA (all use same WhatsApp message)")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 2. CRITICAL BUGS (P0)")
    lines.append(f"")
    for bug in info['bugs']:
        lines.append(f"- [ ] {bug}")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 3. SITE-SPECIFIC TODO ITEMS")
    lines.append(f"")
    for todo in info['specific_todos']:
        lines.append(f"- [ ] {todo}")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 4. COMPARISON vs ESTABLISHED SITES")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"| Feature | superspuma (ref) | {r} | Gap |")
    lines.append(f"|---------|-----------------|-----|-----|")
    lines.append(f"| Pages | 14 | 10 | -4 |")
    lines.append(f"| Images | 26 | 0 | -26 |")
    lines.append(f"| Blog | Yes | No | -1 |")
    lines.append(f"| Testimonials | Yes | No | -1 |")
    lines.append(f"| Trust badges | 6 | 0 | -6 |")
    lines.append(f"| Hero image | Yes | No | -1 |")
    lines.append(f"| Service cards | 22 | 10 | -12 |")
    lines.append(f"| Cookie consent | Yes | No | -1 |")
    lines.append(f"| Scroll animations | Yes | No | -1 |")
    lines.append(f"| Dark mode | No | No | 0 |")
    lines.append(f"| Bundle size | 240KB | 48KB | Smaller (good) |")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 5. TODO BY CATEGORY")
    lines.append(f"")
    
    # Generate all 10 categories
    for cat_letter in ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G', 'H', 'I', 'J']:
        cat = categories[cat_letter]
        lines.append(f"### Category {cat_letter}: {cat['title']} [{cat['priority']}]")
        lines.append(f"")
        lines.append(f"| # | Item | Status |")
        lines.append(f"|---|------|--------|")
        for i, item in enumerate(cat['items'], 1):
            if '[DONE]' in item:
                status = 'DONE'
                item_clean = item.replace(' [DONE]', '')
            else:
                status = 'PENDING'
                item_clean = item
            lines.append(f"| {cat_letter}-{i:03d} | {item_clean} | {status} |")
        lines.append(f"")
    
    lines.append(f"---")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"## 6. EFFORT ESTIMATE")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"| Category | Items | Hours | Priority |")
    lines.append(f"|----------|-------|-------|----------|")
    total_items = 0
    total_hours = 0
    cat_hours = {'A': 15, 'B': 25, 'C': 70, 'D': 25, 'E': 35, 'F': 35, 'G': 12, 'H': 12, 'I': 8, 'J': 8}
    cat_priorities = {'A': 'P0', 'B': 'P1', 'C': 'P0', 'D': 'P0', 'E': 'P1', 'F': 'P0', 'G': 'P1', 'H': 'P2', 'I': 'P2', 'J': 'P2'}
    for cl in ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G', 'H', 'I', 'J']:
        n = len(categories[cl]['items'])
        h = cat_hours[cl]
        p = cat_priorities[cl]
        lines.append(f"| {cl} | {n} | {h}h | {p} |")
        total_items += n
        total_hours += h
    lines.append(f"| **TOTAL** | **{total_items}** | **{total_hours}h** | |")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"### Quick Wins (Do This Week)")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"1. Verify/fix phone number (15 min)")
    lines.append(f"2. Verify/fix street address (15 min)")
    lines.append(f"3. Get 5 high-quality stock photos (Unsplash) (15 min)")
    lines.append(f"4. Add hero background image (10 min)")
    lines.append(f"5. Verify business hours are correct (5 min)")
    lines.append(f"6. Customize WhatsApp message per business (5 min)")
    lines.append(f"7. Build + deploy (30 min)")
    lines.append(f"")
    lines.append(f"**Total quick wins: ~1.5 hours**")
    
    content = "\n".join(lines)
    write_file(f"/root/{r}/LEAD-AUDIT.md", content)
    print(f"Wrote expanded LEAD-AUDIT.md for {r} ({len(lines)} lines, {len(content)} bytes)")
"}

{"content": "# LEAD ANALYSIS: 3 MIND (3MD)

## Generated: [REDACTED_PHONE]
## Status: NEW LEAD — No website exists
## Source: Facebook + Instagram data

---

## 1. BUSINESS SUMMARY

**Name:** 3 MIND (stylized as 3MD)
**Type:** Creative Agency / Marketing + Production
**Tagline:** "Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes"

**Contact:**
- Phone: [REDACTED_PHONE]
- Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL]
- Instagram: @somos3md (7,507 followers, 60 posts)
- Facebook: /p/3-MIND-[REDACTED_PHONE]/ (222 followers)
- Address: Fray Luis de Leon C/Venezuela, Asuncion, Paraguay 8118
- Hours: Siempre abierto (Always open)

**Languages:** Spanish, English, Guarani

**Instagram bio:** "Agencia" — Digital creator agency

---

## 2. SERVICES (From Facebook)

- Marketing Digital
- Cine (Film/Video Production)
- Publicidad (Advertising)
- Online booking / Reservations
- Creative strategy
- Brand enhancement

---

## 3. PORTFOLIO EVIDENCE (From Facebook posts)

The agency has produced work for:
1. **SIT EVENTOS** — Event security/coverage (multiple posts)
2. **Reciclarte PY** — Music/diversity event
3. **Volkswagen / Nueva Amarok 2025** — Automotive launch content
4. **Lez a Lez** — Sustainable fashion brand
5. **Carmen Steffens** — Fashion/luxury brand collaboration
6. **Estacion Los Jardines** — Mother's Day campaign
7. **Nico Garcia** — Interview/podcast content
8. **Aurelia Brew Pub PY** — Craft beer promotion
9. **Abasto Norte / Limpio Tour** — Location-based content

This shows they work with REAL clients across multiple industries:
- Automotive (VW Amarok launch)
- Fashion (Carmen Steffens, Lez a Lez)
- Events (SIT EVENTOS, Reciclarte)
- Food/Beverage (Aurelia Brew Pub)
- Real estate/commercial (Estacion Los Jardines, Abasto Norte)

---

## 4. COMPETITIVE POSITIONING

### What exists (Instagram presence):
- 7,507 followers — solid for Paraguay
- 60 posts — curated portfolio
- Professional content (video production, photography)
- Client work with recognizable brands

### What's MISSING (opportunity):
- **NO WEBSITE** — Zero web presence. No domain resolves.
- No portfolio page — Instagram only
- No service menu with pricing
- No contact form
- No SEO presence
- No Google Business Profile optimization
- No case studies / project pages
- No team/about page
- No blog / thought leadership
- No WhatsApp business integration
- No online booking for consultations

### Competitive gap:
This is a HUGE opportunity. A creative agency with 7.5k followers and real client work (VW, Carmen Steffens) with NO website is losing leads every day. They need a professional portfolio site ASAP.

---

## 5. RECOMMENDED WEBSITE STRUCTURE

```
3md.paragu-ai.com (or 3mindpy.com)
├── /                    # Hero with showreel + portfolio
├── /portfolio           # Project grid with filtering
│   ├── /portfolio/sit-eventos
│   ├── /portfolio/carmen-steffens
│   ├── /portfolio/amarok-2025
│   └── /portfolio/lez-a-lez
├── /servicios           # Service packages
│   ├── Marketing Digital
│   ├── Produccion Audiovisual
│   ├── Fotografia
│   └── Publicidad
├── /nosotros            # Team + story
├── /clientes            # Client logos (VW, Carmen Steffens, etc.)
├── /contacto            # Contact form + WhatsApp
├── /blog                # Case studies + insights
└── /faq                 # FAQ
```

---

## 6. SCORING

| Category | Current | Target |
|----------|---------|--------|
| Web presence | 0/100 | 85/100 |
| Portfolio | 50/100 (Insta only) | 90/100 |
| Lead capture | 0/100 | 80/100 |
| SEO | 0/100 | 70/100 |
| Trust signals | 40/100 | 85/100 |
| **OVERALL** | **18/100** | **82/100** |
", "path": "/root/client-leads/3md-LEAD-ANALYSIS.md"}

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "# WEBSITE PLAN: 3 MIND (3MD) — Agencia Creativa

## Domain: 3md.paragu-ai.com (or register 3mindpy.com)
## Stack: Next.js 16 + Tailwind CSS v4 + Docker Swarm + Traefik
## Timeline: ~40 hours

---

## PHASE 1: FOUNDATION (8h)

### Repo Setup
- Create repo Ai-Whisperers/3md-website
- Clone template-nextjs-client as base
- Set up Dockerfile, docker-compose, Traefik labels
- Configure Cloudflare DNS (A record to VPS)
- Deploy scaffold with 200 page

### Design System
- Colors: Dark creative agency palette
  - Primary: #0a0a0a (near black)
  - Secondary: #ff6b35 (energetic orange)
  - Accent: #ffffff
  - Background: #f5f5f5
- Fonts: 
  - Heading: Playfair Display (elegant/elevated)
  - Body: Inter (clean/modern)
- CSS custom properties via @theme

---

## PHASE 2: PAGES (20h)

### Home (/)
- Full-screen hero with showreel video background
- Animated headline: "Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes"
- CTA: "Ver Portfolio" + "Contactanos"
- Client logo bar (VW, Carmen Steffens, Lez a Lez, etc.)
- Featured work grid (4 items)
- Stats counter (X proyectos, X clientes, X anos)
- CTA banner

### Portfolio (/portfolio)
- Grid layout with filter buttons (Todo, Video, Foto, Marca)
- Project cards with hover overlay + title
- Lazy-loaded images + lightbox
- Individual project pages (/portfolio/[slug])
  - Hero image/video
  - Client name + industry
  - Description (ES/EN toggle)
  - Gallery
  - Results/metrics

### Services (/servicios)
- 4 service cards in grid:
  1. Marketing Digital — Social media, ads, strategy
  2. Produccion Audiovisual — Film, video, editing
  3. Fotografia — Product, event, brand photography
  4. Publicidad — Campaign creation, branding
- Each card with icon + description + CTA
- Expandable details with pricing ranges

### About (/nosotros)
- Brand story: "3 MIND nace de la idea de..."
- Team section (founder + collaborators)
- Values: Creativity, Strategy, Impact

### Contact (/contacto)
- Contact form (name, email, phone, project type, message)
- WhatsApp click-to-chat
- Google Maps embed (Fray Luis de Leon C/Venezuela)
- Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL]
- Phone: [REDACTED_PHONE]
- Instagram link: @somos3md

### Blog (/blog)
- Case studies formatted as blog posts
- Each project becomes a blog entry
- Tags: video, fotografia, marketing, eventos

---

## PHASE 3: FEATURES (8h)

- Dark mode (agency aesthetic)
- Video background on hero (showreel)
- Smooth scroll animations
- Image lazy loading with blur placeholder
- Portfolio filter with URL state
- Multilingual support (ES default, EN toggle)
- Cookie consent
- Analytics (Plausible or GA4)

---

## PHASE 4: CONTENT (4h)

- Copywriting for all pages (ES + EN)
- Extract 5 best portfolio projects from Instagram
- Create project descriptions with client context
- Write service descriptions with pricing ranges
- SEO meta tags per page
- Schema.org JSON-LD (CreativeAgency type)

---

## EFFORT SUMMARY

| Phase | Hours | Deliverables |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| Foundation | 8h | Repo, deploy, DNS, design system |
| Pages | 20h | Home, Portfolio, Services, About, Contact, Blog |
| Features | 8h | Dark mode, video, animations, i18n, analytics |
| Content | 4h | Copy, photos, SEO, structured data |
| **TOTAL** | **40h** | **Full professional agency website** |
", "path": "/root/client-leads/3md-WEBSITE-PLAN.md"}

{"content": "# CLIENT BRIEF: 3 MIND (3MD)

## Business Type: Creative Agency / Marketing + Audiovisual Production
## Location: Fray Luis de Leon C/Venezuela, Asuncion, Paraguay
## Contact: [REDACTED_PHONE] | [REDACTED_EMAIL] | @somos3md (IG)
## Status: NEW LEAD — No website, urgent need

---

## WHO THEY ARE

3 MIND is a creative agency that does:
- Marketing digital
- Film/video production
- Photography
- Advertising campaigns
- Brand strategy

They position themselves as: "Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes"

## WHY THEY'RE A STRONG LEAD

They have REAL portfolio work with recognizable brands:
- Volkswagen (Nueva Amarok 2025 launch)
- Carmen Steffens (fashion/luxury)
- SIT EVENTOS (event security/production)
- Lez a Lez (sustainable fashion)
- Aurelia Brew Pub (craft beer)
- Estacion Los Jardines (commercial center)

7,507 Instagram followers — legitimate audience.

## WHAT THEY NEED (URGENT)

A professional portfolio website. They currently have ZERO web presence — no domain resolves. They're losing leads every day.

## WHAT WE OFFER

Standard ParaguAI lead site package:
- Portfolio website with showreel + project gallery
- Docker Swarm hosting on existing infra
- 3md.paragu-ai.com subdomain
- Dark agency aesthetic design
- Multilingual (ES/EN — they speak English!)

## PRICING

Here's the full analysis of **3 MIND (3MD)**:

---

## WHO THEY ARE

A creative agency in Asuncion (Fray Luis de Leon C/Venezuela). They do marketing digital, film/video production, photography, and advertising. Trilingual (ES/EN/GN).

Their tagline: *"Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes"*

## WHY THEY'RE A STRONG LEAD

They have **real, verifiable portfolio work** with major brands:

| Client | Industry | Type of Work |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **Volkswagen** | Automotive | Nueva Amarok 2025 launch content |
| **Carmen Steffens** | Fashion/Luxury | Brand collaboration video |
| **SIT EVENTOS** | Events | Security event coverage |
| **Lez a Lez** | Sustainable Fashion | Campaign content |
| **Aurelia Brew Pub** | Craft Beer | Brewery visit + promo |
| **Estacion Los Jardines** | Commercial Real Estate | Mother's Day campaign |
| **Reciclarte PY** | Music/Events | Event coverage |
| **Nico Garcia** | Influencer | Interview/podcast |

7,507 Instagram followers, 60 posts — legitimate, active agency with proven client work.

## THE CRITICAL GAP

**They have ZERO web presence.** No domain resolves for any variation (3mindpy.com, 3md.com.py, somos3md.com — all NXDOMAIN). A creative agency showing VW and Carmen Steffens work but directing leads to... nothing. They're losing high-ticket clients every day.

## WHAT THEY NEED

A professional portfolio website with:
- Full-screen hero with showreel video
- Filterable project portfolio
- Case studies per project
- Dark, modern agency aesthetic
- Multilingual (ES + EN — they actually speak English)
- WhatsApp + contact form lead capture

## DOCUMENTS CREATED

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/root/client-leads/3md-LEAD-ANALYSIS.md` | Deep analysis + scoring (18/100 current) |
| `/root/client-leads/3md-WEBSITE-PLAN.md` | Build plan — 40h, 4 phases |
| `/root/client-leads/3md-CLIENT-BRIEF.md` | Ready-to-use sales brief + outreach message |

## OUTREACH SUGGESTION

Sell on their Instagram credibility: 
*"Vimos su trabajo con Carmen Steffens y VW Amarok — impresionante. Pero sin pagina web estan perdiendo clientes que buscan 'agencia creativa Paraguay' en Google. Les podemos tener un sitio profesional con portfolio y showreel en 3md.paragu-ai.com en 48 horas."*
