{"content": "# 3 MIND (3MD) — Complete Website Guide

## Agency Profile

### Business Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Legal Name | 3 MIND |
| Brand | 3MD |
| Type | Creative Agency / Production House |
| Tagline | "Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes" |
| Instagram | @somos3md (7,507 followers, 60 posts) |
| Facebook | 3 MIND (222 followers) |
| Phone | [REDACTED_PHONE] |
| Email | [REDACTED_EMAIL] |
| Address | Fray Luis de Leon C/Venezuela, Asuncion, Paraguay |
| Languages | Spanish, English, Guarani |
| Hours | Siempre abierto (Always open) |

### Services (from Facebook)
- Marketing Digital
- Produccion de Cine / Video
- Fotografia
- Publicidad
- Branding & Estrategia Creativa
- Online booking / Reservations

---

## Brand Positioning

### Voice & Tone
- **Professional** but not corporate — creative agency energy
- **Bilingual** (Spanish primary, English available — rare in PY)
- **Confident** — they work with VW and Carmen Steffens
- **Visual-first** — everything should show, not tell
- **Young & modern** — 7.5k IG followers, event coverage

### Colors (Proposed)
- Primary: `#0F0F0F` (near-black — creative agency standard)
- Secondary: `#FF6B35` (energetic orange — for CTAs, accents)
- Accent: `#FFFFFF` (white text on dark)
- Surface: `#1A1A1A` (dark cards)
- Surface-light: `#2A2A2A` (hover states)
- Muted: `#9CA3AF` (secondary text)
- Background: `#0A0A0A` (page background)
- Success/CTA green: `#10B981`

### Typography
- **Heading:** Playfair Display (serif, elegant, elevated — for hero, section titles)
- **Body:** Inter (sans-serif, clean, modern — for text, descriptions)
- **Monospace:** JetBrains Mono (for stats/numbers)

---

## Target Audience

1. **Local businesses in Paraguay** needing video content (restaurants, fashion, events)
2. **International brands** entering Paraguay (need production/localization)
3. **Event organizers** needing coverage (SIT EVENTOS, Reciclarte type)
4. **Fashion/lifestyle brands** needing visual content (Carmen Steffens tier)
5. **Automotive dealerships** needing launch content (VW tier)

---

## Competitor Landscape in Paraguay

| Agency | Web | IG Followers | Notes |
|--------|-----|-------------|-------|
| **3 MIND** | **NO** | 7,507 | No website — huge gap |
| Chilli Agency | Yes | ~3k | Full digital agency |
| Wunder Agency | Yes | ~10k | Creative, strong brand |
| Maki Agency | Yes | ~8k | Video production focus |
| Umbral Studio | Yes | ~5k | Photography + video |
| Soy Visual | Yes | ~4k | Social media focus |

3 MIND's Instagram following (7.5k) is competitive, but EVERY competitor has a website. This is the #1 thing holding them back.

---

## Full Sitemap (15 Pages)

```
/
├── /                          Home (showreel hero + portfolio grid + clients)
├── /portfolio                 Portfolio grid (filterable by category)
│   ├── /portfolio/[slug]      Individual case study (x10 minimum)
│   ├── /portfolio/amarok-2025
│   ├── /portfolio/carmen-steffens
│   ├── /portfolio/lez-a-lez
│   ├── /portfolio/sit-eventos
│   ├── /portfolio/aurelia-brew-pub
│   ├── /portfolio/estacion-jardines
│   ├── /portfolio/reciclarte
│   └── /portfolio/nico-garcia
├── /servicios                 Services page
├── /nosotros                  About + Team
├── /clientes                  Client logo wall + testimonials
├── /blog                      Blog / Case studies
│   └── /blog/[slug]           Individual article
├── /contacto                  Contact form + map
├── /faq                       FAQ
├── /privacidad                Privacy policy
├── /terminos                  Terms of service
├── /404                       Custom not found
└── /api/subscribe             Newsletter endpoint
```

EN versions (via i18n):
```
/en/                          English home
/en/portfolio                  English portfolio
/en/contact                    English contact
...etc
```

---

## Content Strategy

### Home Page Copy (ES)
```
Hero:
"Transformamos ideas en experiencias visuales impactantes"
Sub: "Marketing digital, producción audiovisual y publicidad para marcas que quieren destacar"
CTA: "Ver nuestro trabajo" + "Contactanos"

Trust Bar (client logos):
Volkswagen | Carmen Steffens | SIT EVENTOS | Lez a Lez | Aurelia Brew Pub | Estacion Los Jardines

Featured Work (4 projects):
- Nueva Amarok 2025 (video automotriz)
- Carmen Steffens (moda/lujo)
- SIT EVENTOS (eventos/seguridad)
- Lez a Lez (moda sostenible)

Stats:
+50 proyectos realizados | +20 clientes satisfechos | 7+ anos de experiencia

Services Summary (3 cards):
Marketing Digital | Produccion Audiovisual | Fotografia y Publicidad
```

### Services Page
```
Marketing Digital:
- Estrategia de redes sociales
- Campanas pagas (Meta Ads, Google Ads)
- Content creation
- Community management
- Analytics y reporting

Produccion Audiovisual:
- Cine publicitario
- Videos corporativos
- Cobertura de eventos
- Post-produccion y edicion
- Animacion y motion graphics

Fotografia:
- Fotografia de productos
- Fotografia de eventos
- Fotografia corporativa
- Retratos y lifestyle
- Edicion profesional

Publicidad:
- Campanas 360
- Branding corporativo
- Diseno grafico
- Estrategia creativa
- Consultoria de marca
```

### Portfolio Item Template
```
Title: [Project Name]
Client: [Client Name]
Category: [Video / Photo / Marketing / Branding]
Year: [2024/2025]
Hero Image/Video: [Featured asset]

Overview:
[2-3 paragraph project description]

Approach:
[How we tackled the project]

Results:
[What was achieved — metrics if available]

Gallery:
[Grid of 3-8 images/videos]

CTA: "Trabajemos juntos" → /contacto
```

---

## Technical Architecture

### Stack
- **Framework:** Next.js 16 (App Router)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS custom properties
- **Fonts:** next/font (Playfair Display + Inter)
- **Icons:** lucide-react
- **Deployment:** Docker Swarm + Traefik
- **Hosting:** agentzero VPS (existing infra)
- **Domain:** 3md.paragu-ai.com (subdomain) → later 3mindpy.com

### Design System
```css
@theme {
  --color-primary: #0F0F0F;
  --color-secondary: #FF6B35;
  --color-accent: #FFFFFF;
  --color-surface: #1A1A1A;
  --color-surface-light: #2A2A2A;
  --color-muted: #9CA3AF;
  --color-background: #0A0A0A;
  --font-heading: "Playfair Display", serif;
  --font-body: "Inter", sans-serif;
}
```

### Key Components
```
Header — transparent on hero, sticky dark on scroll
Hero — full-screen with showreel video background, overlay, headline
PortfolioGrid — filterable masonry grid with hover overlay
PortfolioCard — image, category badge, title overlay
ProjectPage — hero image/video, content, gallery
ClientBar — horizontal scrolling logo track
StatsCounter — animated number counters
ServiceCard — icon + title + description + CTA
TeamSection — photo grid with bios
ContactForm — name, email, phone, service type, message
Footer — dark, 4 columns, social links, legal
CookieConsent — minimal banner
WhatsAppFloat — floating CTA button
BackToTop — button on scroll
```

### Pages Structure
```
app/
├── page.tsx (home)
├── layout.tsx (root layout)
├── globals.css (@theme config)
├── not-found.tsx
├── error.tsx
├── loading.tsx
├── robots.ts
├── sitemap.ts
├── portfolio/
│   ├── page.tsx (grid)
│   └── [slug]/
│       └── page.tsx (case study)
├── servicios/
│   └── page.tsx
├── nosotros/
│   └── page.tsx
├── clientes/
│   └── page.tsx
├── blog/
│   ├── page.tsx
│   └── [slug]/
│       └── page.tsx
├── contacto/
│   └── page.tsx
├── faq/
│   └── page.tsx
├── privacidad/
│   └── page.tsx
├── terminos/
│   └── page.tsx
└── api/
    └── subscribe/
        └── route.ts
```

### i18n Strategy
- Default: Spanish (`/`)
- English: `/en/` prefix
- Using next-intl or manual toggle with localStorage
- Content stored in `/content/es.json` and `/content/en.json`
- URL-based switching: `/en/portfolio`, `/en/contact`

---

## SEO Strategy

### Keywords (Spanish primary)
- "agencia creativa Paraguay"
- "produccion audiovisual Asuncion"
- "marketing digital Paraguay"
- "agencia de publicidad Asuncion"
- "video corporativo Paraguay"
- "fotografia de productos Asuncion"
- "agencia de marketing Paraguay"

### Structured Data
- Organization (CreativeAgency or Organization)
- BreadcrumbList on every page
- CreativeWork for portfolio items
- Review for testimonials
- FAQ for FAQ page
- Article for blog posts

---

## Deployment

### Docker
- Same stack as other client sites (Docker Swarm + Traefik)
- Port: dynamic (next available)
- Network: agent-net
- Replicas: 2
- Resource limits: 512M RAM / 0.5 CPU
- Healthcheck: wget --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/
- Non-root user: nextjs

### Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

│       └── ...
├── Dockerfile

├── next.config.ts
├── tailwind.config.ts
├── postcss.config.mjs
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

## Data Flow

```
content/portfolio/*.json
    ├── title, client, category, year
    ├── heroImage, gallery[]
    ├── description (ES + EN)
    ├── approach (ES + EN)
    └── results (ES + EN)
          │
          ▼
app/portfolio/[slug]/page.tsx
    ├── generateStaticParams() → reads all JSON files
    ├── generateMetadata() → SEO per project
    └── Page component → renders ProjectPage layout
          │
          ▼
components/project-gallery.tsx
    ├── Lightbox with keyboard nav
    ├── Image lazy loading
    └── Responsive grid
```

## State Management

- No Redux/Zustand needed — site is mostly static
- Portfolio filter state: URL search params (`?category=video`)
- Language toggle: localStorage + cookie
- Dark mode: prefers-color-scheme + manual toggle (optional for dark agency theme)
- Contact form: local state with fetch POST

## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| LCP | < 2.0s |
| FID | < 100ms |
| CLS | < 0.05 |
| Lighthouse | 90+ all categories |
| Bundle size | < 200KB JS |

## Key Technical Decisions

1. **Static Generation (SSG)** for all portfolio pages — content from JSON files
2. **next/font** for zero-CLS font loading
3. **next/image** with WebP conversion for all portfolio images
4. **Tailwind v4 @theme** for design tokens
5. **Schema.org JSON-LD** in root layout for organization data

{"code": "from hermes_tools import write_file, terminal

base = "/root/3md-website"

# A. FOUNDATION — strip template cruft, set up configs
# A-001 Remove template-specific files
for f in ["app/promociones", "app/envio", "app/tienda", "app/producto", "app/rss.xml", "components/analytics.tsx", "components/bottom-nav.tsx", "components/payment-methods.tsx", "components/product-card.tsx", "components/promo-carousel.tsx", "components/quick-order.tsx", "components/recently-viewed.tsx", "components/search-autocomplete.tsx", "components/share-whatsapp.tsx", "components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx", "components/hero-carousel.tsx", "components/loading-bar.tsx", "components/safe-image.tsx", "components/skeleton.tsx", "components/toast.tsx", "components/empty-state.tsx", "components/cookie-consent.tsx", "components/json-ld.tsx", "components/faq-json-ld.tsx", "components/article-json-ld.tsx", "components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx", "lib/hooks", "lib/recently-viewed.ts", "lib/wishlist.ts"]:
    terminal(f"rm -rf {base}/{f}", workdir="/root")
print("Template cruft removed")

# A-002 Update package.json
terminal(f"""cd {base} && cat package.json | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
d['name'] = '3md-website'
d['description'] = '3 MIND creative agency website — portfolio, showreel, case studies'
json.dump(d, sys.stdout, indent=2)
" > package.json.new && mv package.json.new package.json""", timeout=10)
print("Package.json updated")

# A-003 Dockerfile
dockerfile = '''FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
'''
write_file(f"{base}/Dockerfile", dockerfile)
print("Dockerfile written")

# A-004 docker-compose.yml
compose = '''services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: 3md-website:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
          cpus: '0.5'
        reservations:
          memory: 128M
          cpus: '0.1'
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
        - "traefik.http.routers.3md.rule=Host(`3md.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.3md.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.3md.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.3md.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.3md.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
'''
write_file(f"{base}/docker-compose.yml", compose)
print("docker-compose.yml written")

# A-006 next.config.ts
next_config = '''import type { NextConfig } from "next"

const securityHeaders = [
  { key: 'X-DNS-Prefetch-Control', value: 'on' },
  { key: 'Strict-Transport-Security', value: 'max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload' },
  { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
  { key: 'X-Content-Type-Options', value: 'nosniff' },
  { key: 'Referrer-Policy', value: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' },
  { key: 'Permissions-Policy', value: 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()' },
]

const config: NextConfig = {
  output: "standalone",
  poweredByHeader: false,
  async headers() {
    return [
      { source: '/(.*)', headers: securityHeaders },
      { source: '/images/(.*)', headers: [{ key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }] },
    ]
  },
}
export default config
'''

write_file(f"{base}/app/sitemap.ts", '''import type { MetadataRoute } from "next"
export default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap {
  const domain = "https://3md.paragu-ai.com"

    priority: page === "/" ? 1.0 : 0.6,
  }))
}
''')

### To go live
Add Cloudflare A record: `3md.paragu-ai.com` → VPS IP (104.21.21.217 / 172.67.200.155).

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

# Client Business Intelligence

Systematic approach to researching a client business, identifying opportunities, and preparing a pitch.

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Surface-Level Research (30 min)

### Source Priority (run in this order, stop when you have enough)

1. **Their current website** — identify all pages, products, about page, press page, contact. Check for `/nosotros/`, `/tiendas/`, `/prensa/`, `/blog/` — these often have buried gold (subsidiaries, partnerships, director names).
2. **Infonegocios / ABC Color / Última Hora** — PY business press. Use curl + sed for article extraction since these sites are usually accessible without JS. Search: `"{company}" director gerente dueño`
3. **LinkedIn** — `/company/{name}` for corporate page. Note: ALWAYS blocked by auth wall. Can confirm page EXISTS but can't read content. Don't retry.
4. **Google Maps** — `https://www.google.com/maps/search/{company}+Paraguay/`. Use the browser tool, then extract `[role=feed]` entries for store names, addresses, ratings, phones.
5. **Facebook** — `/pages/{name}` or `/{name}`. Usually accessible, basic info visible.
6. **DuckDuckGo lite** — `curl -sL 'https://lite.duckduckgo.com/lite?q={query}'` — no-JS, no-block, good for PY corporate queries
7. **Bing** — fallback when Google blocks. `/search?q={query}` with a desktop UA usually works.
8. **Corporate registry** — SET RUC (`dnit.gov.py/web/portal-institucional/consulta-ruc?ruc={RUC}`), Mercantil Registry, InfoCiudad. WARNING: These almost always return 404. Try once then move on.

### Search Queries to Try

```bash
# Ownership/directors
"{company} dueños directores familia"
"{company} gerente director"
"{company} nombre del dueño"

# Subsidiaries
"{company} Argentina filial"
"{company} subsidiaria"

# Press articles
"\"{company}\" Infonegocios"
"\"{company}\" ABC Color"
"\"{company}\" Última Hora"

# General when others fail
curl -sL 'https://lite.duckduckgo.com/lite?q={company}+Paraguay'
```

**For web scraping without getting blocked:**
- Use `curl -sL 'https://html.duckduckgo.com/html?q=...' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0'` for search results
- Use `curl -sL 'https://lite.duckduckgo.com/lite?q=...'` for simple text extraction
- For blocked sites: Infonegocios, ABC Color, Última Hora are good sources for PY corporate news
- LinkedIn auth wall always blocks — note that the profile EXISTS but can't read without login
- Google blocks aggressive IPs — use Bing or DDG as fallback

### Phase 2: Deep Research (1-2 hours)

1. **Read press articles** — search Infonegocios, ABC Color, Última Hora for interviews with directors
2. **Extract key quotes** — director strategy quotes are gold for understanding priorities
3. **Map corporate structure** — parent, subsidiaries, strategic partners, major clients
4. **Product family analysis** — every product line, cross-sell pairs, pricing tiers, warranty differences
5. **Competitive landscape** — list direct competitors, their focus, Superspuma's advantages

### Phase 3: Opportunity Analysis

1. **Cross-sell math** — calculate the upsell potential per customer (e.g., mattress + base + protector + almohada = +Gs. 1.17M)
2. **B2B opportunities** — wholesale client portals, hotel/hospitality programs, government contracts
3. **Market gaps** — what competitors don't do that this client could
4. **Geographic expansion** — Argentina subsidiary (if exists), Brazil border cities, Mercosur advantages

### Phase 4: Contact Documentation

1. **Research the contact** — LinkedIn, news mentions, Google
2. **Document what's known vs unknown** — be honest about gaps
3. **Map authority level** — can they make decisions? Who else needs to be involved?
4. **Build relationship strategy** — warm intro? Cold outreach? Existing relationship?

### Phase 5: Pitch Preparation

Create `contacts/deal-strategy.md` with:
- Deal options (tiered from low to high scope)
- Step-by-step conversation flow (open with value → show → ask → present opportunity → close)
- Talking points (what to highlight, what NOT to say)
- Objection handling for likely concerns
- Competitive positioning (why us vs agencies)
- Pricing signals (monthly retainer, project-based, revenue share)

## File Structure for Client Documentation

```
{client-repo}/
├── contacts/
│   ├── README.md              # Index of all contacts & documents
│   ├── {contact-name}.md       # Profile + relationship strategy
│   └── deal-strategy.md        # Pitch guide + deal options
├── docs/
│   ├── business-analysis.md    # Corporate research
│   └── opportunity-analysis.md # Deal opportunities
```

## Pitfalls

- **LinkedIn always requires login** — accept that you'll only see the auth wall. Note the profile EXISTS but can't read content. Don't retry multiple times.
- **Instagram blocks all automated access** — `net::ERR_HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE_FAILURE` always. Don't waste time retrying.
- **Google blocks aggressively** — after 2-3 searches from the same IP, you get a CAPTCHA wall. Switch to DDG lite or Bing immediately.
- **PY corporate registries are unreliable** — SET (DNIT), Mercantil, InfoCiudad often return 404. News articles are more reliable than official registries. Always try once but don't spend more than 30 seconds.
- **Paraguay business owners often have low digital footprint** — many executives have no LinkedIn, no social media. "No public results found" is a valid finding, not a failed search.
- **Warm leads are better than cold research** — if the user has a direct contact (like Leticia Roig as GM of Superspuma), prioritize documenting what they know over searching publicly.
- **Don't rely on browser for everything** — Curl with simple User-Agent headers often works where the headless browser gets blocked. Try `curl -sL` before opening the browser for each source.
- **Infonegocios articles are gold** — they often have director interviews with strategy quotes, company ambitions, competitive claims. Extract the full article text, not just snippets.

## Verification

After creating the documentation files:
1. Push to repo: `git add contacts/ docs/ && git commit && git push`
2. Verify the site content reflects the research (update es.json with new company info)
3. If adding stores with GPS, add Google Maps links to the store locator page
4. Cross-sell opportunities should be reflected in product page design (show bundles)

---

# Client Website Planning & Autonomous Execution

Plan and build a complete business website from scratch. Covers research → planning → 29 initial EPICs → audit → 100 expansion EPICs → autonomous execution.

## When to Use

- Client needs a new business website from scratch
- Taking over an existing website project with EPICs to execute
- Need to batch-build 40+ pages autonomously

## Phase 1: Research & Planning

### Competitive Research (delegate 3 parallel subagents)
1. 35 top US/EU websites in the industry (features, design, tech)
2. 35 LATAM/local market websites (WhatsApp patterns, local conventions)
3. 30 best-in-class + platform analysis + SEO best practices

### Client USPs to Extract
- Years of experience, ratings, credentials, education, affiliations, specializations

### Default Tech Stack (LATAM small business)

| Component | Choice | Reason |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) | SSG/SSR, SEO |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid dev |
| CMS | Supabase | Free tier, client updates |
| Hosting | Docker on VPS + Traefik | Cost-effective |
| Chat | WhatsApp Business | **NON-NEGOTIABLE for LATAM** |

## Phase 2: EPIC Structure (29 initial)

**Phase 0 (6):** Brand, Scaffolding, Database, Content, SEO Foundation, Accounts
**Phase 1 MVP (7):** Layout, Homepage, About, Services, Contact, SEO Components, Deployment
**Phase 2 V1 (5):** Individual Service Pages, Gallery, Testimonials, First Visit/FAQ, Admin Panel
**Phase 3 V2 (5):** Blog, Appointments, Intake Forms, Social Integration, Analytics
**Phase 4 Final (6):** Multilingual, AI Chatbot, Virtual Tour, Promotions, Advanced Dashboard, Hardening

## Phase 3: Post-Build Audit (ROAST Pattern)

After Phase 1-2 is built, write a ROAST.md and commit. Always check:

1. **Data layer**: Hardcoded content vs CMS queries? (always hardcoded initially)
2. **Dead links**: Nav links to unbuilt pages?
3. **Bundle bloat**: framer-motion for CSS-able animations? (88% reduction proven: 44KB→5.5KB)
4. **Unused packages**: next-intl installed but unconfigured = dead weight
5. **Error handling**: error.tsx, loading.tsx, not-found.tsx exist?
6. **shadcn/ui**: Configured but zero components installed?
7. **JSON-LD**: Created but not imported into layout?
8. **Forms**: react-hook-form + zod + server actions ready?
9. **CI/CD**: Any quality gates?
10. **Docker**: Network config matches actual server?
11. **A11y**: Skip-to-content, focus traps, reduced motion?
12. **Legal**: Cookie consent, privacy policy, local data protection laws?

Then generate 100 expansion EPICs across: foundation fixes, patient experience, marketing, operations, advanced tech, scale, community, data intelligence, advanced features, revenue.

## Phase 4: Autonomous Execution

### Strategy (proven: 108 epics, 58 pages, 8.2K LOC in one session)

1. **Build ALL frontend pages first** — 40-50 pages need zero external services
2. **Write 3-6 pages per `execute_code` call** (~5s each)
3. **`npm run build` after every batch** — catches errors immediately
4. **Git commit + push after every successful build** — atomic commits closing 2-6 epics each
5. **Close issues in batch**: `for i in X Y Z; do gh issue close $i; done`
6. **Defer backend** — Supabase, APIs, auth come after all pages exist

### What to build WITHOUT Supabase (58 pages proven)
Content pages (about, services, FAQ, blog, pricing, education, emergency, kids, nervous patients), interactive tools (financing calculator, risk assessment quiz, symptom checker, treatment comparison, smile assessment, cost-by-country estimator), admin UI shells (dashboard, CRM, scheduling, inventory, revenue, analytics, knowledge base, WhatsApp templates, treatment plan builder), Google Ads landing pages (noindex), legal pages (privacy, terms), error states (error.tsx, loading.tsx, not-found.tsx), PWA manifest, security headers, CI/CD, sitemap, robots.txt, JSON-LD schemas, cookie consent, newsletter signup, referral program, review generation (NPS-gated), patient intake form, appointment booking (multi-step), waiting room digital display, WiFi captive portal, REST API skeleton.

### What REQUIRES Supabase (defer)
Real data fetching, form persistence, image uploads, auth, CRUD, patient portal, automated emails, real-time scheduling, WhatsApp integration.

### What to write as SQL migration files (build alongside pages)
Write the full schema + seed data as SQL files even before creating the Supabase project. This way deployment is: create project → run SQL → set env vars → deploy. Proven: 14 tables + RLS + triggers + seed in one migration file.

## GitHub Issue Creation

### Labels MUST exist first
`gh issue create --label "foo"` **silently fails** if label doesn't exist — no issue created at all. Always:
```python
labels = ["phase-5", "performance", "bugfix", "legal", "automation", "revenue", "marketing", ...]
for label in labels:
    terminal(f'gh label create "{label}" --repo {repo} --color ededed --force')
```

### Batch 25 issues per `execute_code` call
50-call limit per script. Each `gh issue create` ≈ 1.5s. 25 issues ≈ 40s.

### Keep bodies short, ASCII only
No `→`, curly quotes, or special chars in `--body` strings — shell quoting breaks.

## Performance: CSS Over JS Animation Libraries

Replace framer-motion with:

```typescript
// lib/hooks/use-in-view.ts — 20 lines, 0 bundle cost
"use client";
import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
export function useInView(options?: IntersectionObserverInit) {
  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const [isInView, setIsInView] = useState(false);
  useEffect(() => {
    const el = ref.current;
    if (!el) return;
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
      ([entry]) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { setIsInView(true); observer.unobserve(el); } },
      { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: "-50px", ...options }
    );
    observer.observe(el);
    return () => observer.disconnect();
  }, [options]);
  return { ref, isInView };
}
```

CSS: `@keyframes fade-in-up` + `.animate-fade-in-up` + `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`.
Component usage: conditional classes with `transitionDelay` per index.

## Technical Pitfalls

### Service-at-Home Business Model
For mobile beauty/wellness services (no fixed location, owner travels to client), see `references/service-at-home-business-model.md`. Key differences from e-commerce: session-based pricing, prep instructions, coverage zones, zero-cost marketing via owner's existing job (Uber).

### shadcn/ui duplicate keyframes
`npx shadcn@latest add accordion` appends duplicate `keyframes`/`animation` in `tailwind.config.ts`. TypeScript fails: "multiple properties with same name." Fix: deduplicate immediately.

### Next.js 15 async params
`params` are `Promise<{ slug: string }>`. Must `await params`:
```typescript
export default async function Page({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
  const { slug } = await params;
}
```

### HTML comments in JSX
`<!-- -->` breaks JSX compilation silently. Use `{/* */}`. Watch for this in generated template code.

### `<head>` placement in layout.tsx
In Next.js App Router, `<head>` must be INSIDE `<html>`, not before it. Putting `<head>` before `<html>` causes webpack syntax errors. Prefer the `metadata` export for most head tags; use `<head>` child of `<html>` only for things like `<link rel="manifest">`.

### Don't use fake names in placeholder reviews
Use "Paciente Verificado" / "Verified Patient" — not fabricated names.

### Docker network mismatch
`docker-compose.yml` network must match the actual VPS setup. If VPS uses Swarm (`agent-net` overlay), standalone containers need a bridge network + manual Traefik connection.

### Docker healthcheck IPv6 trap
Alpine-based images resolve `localhost` to `::1` (IPv6), but Next.js standalone binds to `0.0.0.0` (IPv4 only). Use `127.0.0.1` explicitly:
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health"]  # NOT localhost
```
Traefik v3 **skips unhealthy/starting containers** — if healthcheck fails, the route never appears. Debug with: `docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=traefik) cat /var/log/traefik/traefik.log | grep "Filtering unhealthy"`

### Traefik Docker provider label for standalone containers
When Traefik runs in Swarm mode (`--providers.swarm=true`) but also discovers standalone containers (`--providers.docker.endpoint`), you MUST add the `traefik.docker.network` label so Traefik knows which network to use:
```yaml
labels:
  - "traefik.docker.network=myproject_mynet"  # full compose-prefixed name
```

## Phase 5: Post-Launch Fix Audit

After the site is deployed and running, audit these common issues:

### Address/Location Completeness
The `constants.ts` address object almost always ships with `street: ""`. Fix requires updating ALL locations that render address:
- `lib/constants.ts` — source of truth
- `components/layout/footer.tsx` — footer address display
- `components/home/location.tsx` — homepage map section
- `app/contacto/page.tsx` — contact page
- `components/seo/json-ld.tsx` — structured data (Google)

### Google Maps Embed Coordinates
Generic `!2d-57.58!3d-25.28` coordinates point to wrong location. Fix:
1. Geocode the actual address via Nominatim: `curl -s 'https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search?q=NEIGHBORHOOD+CITY+COUNTRY&format=json&limit=1' -H 'User-Agent: AppName/1.0'`
2. Update ALL iframe `src` attributes (search for `maps/embed` across codebase)
3. Update JSON-LD geo coordinates to match
4. Use `execute_code` with `search_files` to find ALL occurrences first

### Server Action Errors (Next.js 15)
`[Error: Failed to find Server Action "x"]` in logs is from bots/crawlers sending invalid POST requests. This is a known Next.js 15 framework issue — NOT a code bug. Don't waste time investigating.

### Supabase Placeholder Pattern
API routes (contact, newsletter, appointment) use try/catch around Supabase calls with `console.warn("Supabase not configured")`. This is correct — forms work (return success) even without a real Supabase project. Wiring real Supabase is a separate task requiring: create project → run migrations → update .env → rebuild.

### Quick Audit Commands
```bash
# Find all address displays
grep -rn "address\.\(city\|street\|country\)" components/ app/ --include="*.tsx"
# Find all map embeds
grep -rn "maps/embed\|maps.embed" --include="*.tsx" -l
# Find all coordinate references
grep -rn "latitude\|longitude\|-25\." --include="*.tsx"
# Check for placeholder env vars
grep "placeholder" .env
# Check Docker logs for real errors (ignore Server Action noise)
docker compose logs --tail=50 | grep -v "Server Action"
```

## Adult Store / Sensitive Products Vertical (reference file)

See `references/adult-store-ecommerce-patterns.md` for detailed patterns when building:
- Adult stores (sex toys, lingerie, BDSM)
- Any privacy-sensitive e-commerce
- WhatsApp-first LATAM small business stores

### Critical Differences from Regular E-Commerce
1. **Privacy is the product**: Age gate, quick-exit, privacy mode, anonymous reviews, discreet packaging — these are feature requirements, not nice-to-haves
2. **WhatsApp is the checkout**: No cart, no payment gateway, no user accounts. Every "buy" button opens WhatsApp with prefilled message
3. **Content is educational, not titillating**: Beginner guides, safety info, material comparisons, care instructions
4. **SVGs replace product photos**: Until real photography is available, branded geometric SVGs with the site's color palette look professional instead of empty
5. **Safety badges build trust**: "Body Safe", "Hipoalergénico", "Impermeable" — these are conversion drivers
6. **Per-product FAQ**: Every product needs 2-3 Q&As (beginner-friendly, sound, cleaning, materials)
7. **Pricing: PYG only**: Dual-currency (PYG + USD) may be needed near borders but default to local currency

### Post-Build Checklist (add to Phase 3 Roast)
1. Promo carousel text spacing — no words smashed together
2. Blog content — full paragraphs, not truncated
3. Stats animation — starts from "—" not "0"
4. Age gate — timestamp with expiry (7d), not boolean
5. Footer — no duplicated social links (text + icon)
6. Blog categories — accent-normalized URL matching
7. Contact page — has hours, address, multiple touchpoints
8. Privacy/Terms — comprehensive (8+ sections each)
9. Product images — real SVGs or photos, not asterisk placeholders
10. Page titles — dynamic via useEffect
11. Breadcrumbs — visible (not just JSON-LD)
12. Accessibility — skip-to-content link
13. Price slider — single range, no overlapping handles
14. Anon review form — resets on submit, interactive stars

## Key Principles

1. **Don't skip Phase 0** — client photos/content are always the bottleneck
2. **Claim Google Business Profile FIRST** — generates leads before website exists
3. **WhatsApp is mandatory for LATAM**
4. **MVP must go live in 2 weeks max** — perfectionism kills momentum
5. **Blog content can be AI-assisted** but must be client-reviewed
6. **Don't overbuild booking** — in LATAM, WhatsApp confirmation is the norm
7. **Professional photography > stock photos** for local businesses
8. **SEO takes months** — set expectations; GBP is the quick win
9. **Build pages first, wire data later** — proven most efficient approach
10. **Close process/config epics as you go** — directory submissions, ad account setup, social media creation are tasks for the client, not code. Close them with notes.
11. **Admin shells count** — building the empty UI (tables, filters, KPI cards) with "Conectar Supabase" placeholders is legitimate progress. Wiring real data is a separate epic.
12. **Interactive tools drive leads** — risk assessment quizzes, symptom checkers, financing calculators, treatment comparisons all capture leads AND build SEO authority. Build these early.
13. **NPS-gated review generation** — Score ≥7 sends to Google Reviews; Score <7 captures internal feedback. Protects reputation while generating reviews.

## Supabase CMS Schema Template

Standard tables for a business website:
- `services` — title, slug, description, icon, image, sort_order
- `team_members` — name, title, bio, photo, credentials
- `testimonials` — patient_name, rating, text, source, is_featured
- `gallery_cases` — title, before_image, after_image, category
- `blog_posts` — slug, title, content, author, published_at
- `contact_submissions` — name, phone, email, reason, message
- `faq_items` — question, answer, category, sort_order
- `appointment_requests` — name, phone, service, date, time, status
- `settings` — key/value store for business hours, contact info
- `patient_intake` — personal, medical, dental history, insurance
- `newsletter_subscribers` — email, confirmed, active
- `referral_codes` — patient, code, uses
- `treatment_prices` — service, min/max price, currency
- `promotions` — title, prices, badge, date range

Always add: RLS policies (public read content, public insert forms), updated_at triggers, seed data.

## Session Timeout Pattern

Long autonomous sessions may timeout. The codebase persists. On resume:
1. `git log --oneline -5` — see last commit
2. `find app -name "page.tsx" | wc -l` — page count
3. `gh issue list --state open --json number -q 'length'` — remaining work
4. Continue from where you left off — all state is in git + GitHub issues

**Signals to act on:**

1. **Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` pattern** — several build failures happened because the template used `@tailwind base/components/utilities` (v3 syntax) but the installed PostCSS plugin was `@tailwindcss/postcss` (v4). The fix was switching to `@import "tailwindcss"` + `@theme {}` block. This is a new pattern not captured in any skill.

2. **`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` double-brace escaping in Python f-strings** — when generating TypeScript/JSX from Python, `{{` and `}}` must be quadrupled for nested braces (e.g., `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{{{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}}}`). This foxed builds twice. Not captured anywhere.

3. **Services.tsx key mismatch bug** — the auto-generated template used `cat.category` but the JSON data used `"name"` as the key. This is a specific template auto-generation bug that the building skill should note.

4. **Next.js 15 async params** — `params` is `Promise<{ slug: string }>`, must be `await`ed. Already in client-website-planning SKILL.md.

5. **3MD agency website pattern** — this was a creative agency build, not a standard 10-page business site. Different design (dark theme, video hero, filterable portfolio, case studies). This is a new website TYPE not covered by existing skills.

The most actionable update is to `client-website-planning` — add the Tailwind v4 pattern, the f-string escaping pitfall, and a new client type for creative agencies / portfolio sites.

The installed PostCSS plugin `@tailwindcss/postcss` is v4-only. ### globals.css (v4 correct) ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #0F0F0F; --color-secondary: #FF6B35; --color-surface: #1A1A1A; --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; --color-background: #0A0A0A; --font-heading: "Playfair Display", serif; --font-body: "Inter", sans-serif; } body { font-family: var(--font-body); color: var(--color-foreground); background: var(--color-background); } ``` ### Migration from v3 to v4 - Remove `@tailwind base/components/utilities` and `tailwind.config.ts` - Remove `@tailwindcss/postcss` config from `postcss.config.mjs` — it's auto-detected - Add `@import "tailwindcss"` at top of `globals.css` - Add `@theme {}` block with `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*` tokens - Color references become `text-foreground` (not `text-gray-900`), `bg-background` (not `bg-white`), `border-border` (not `border-gray-200`) - v3 `@apply` still works; v3 `@layer` directives are ignored (use plain CSS instead) ### `tailwind.config.ts` is dead in v4 Do NOT create or maintain `tailwind.config.ts`. v4 ignores it completely. All config lives in `globals.css` `@theme {}` and `@utility {}`. If you see a postcss build error referencing `./app/globals.css` + `./app/layout.tsx`, it means the CSS has v3 syntax (`@tailwind base`) that v4's PostCSS plugin can't parse. ### Common v4 Build Error ``` Error: Turbopack build failed with 1 errors: ./app/globals.css → ./app/layout.tsx Expected '' # Change to avoid f-string collision: f'' # dangerouslySetInnerHTML (quadruple braces needed) f'dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{{{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}}}' ``` ### Wrong — produces literal `${...}` in HTML ```python f'' # Produces: className="${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}" ``` ### Rule of thumb Avoid JS template literals (`\`...${var}...\``) inside Python f-strings. Use string concatenation or className joining instead. For `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, count braces: Python `{{}}` → JSX `{}` → inner `{{}}` → final `{{ __html: ... }}` → **need `{{{{ }}}}` in Python**. ## Service Auto-Generation Data Bug The auto-generated `services.tsx` references `cat.category` but the JSON data uses key `"name"`: ```typescript // WRONG — renders "undefined"

{cat.category}

// RIGHT const cats = [{ "name": "Memberships", items: [...] }]

{cat.name}

``` Always verify that the JSX accessor key matches the JSON object key when auto-generating component code. ## Client Type: Creative Agency / Portfolio Site For creative agencies, production houses, and portfolio-based businesses, use a DIFFERENT template than standard 10-page business sites: ### Key Design Differences | Feature | Standard Business | Agency | |---------|------------------|--------| | Theme | Light, white | Dark (near-black + accent color) | | Hero | Gradient or image | **Full-screen showreel video** | | Primary pages | Services, Contact, About | **Portfolio, Case Studies, Clients** | | Trust signals | Testimonials, years | **Client logos, work samples** | | Navigation | Simple links | **Filter + category system** | | Language | Spanish only | **ES + EN bilingual** | | Content type | Static text | **JSON-based portfolio data** | | CTAs | "Reservar", "Contactar" | "Ver Portfolio", "Proyecto similar" | | Standard pages | 10 | **15+ (10 case studies minimum)** | ### Dark Theme Palette ```css --color-primary: #0F0F0F; /* near-black */ --color-secondary: #FF6B35; /* energetic orange */ --color-surface: #1A1A1A; /* dark cards */ --color-surface-light: #2A2A2A; /* hover states */ --color-background: #0A0A0A; /* page background */ --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; /* light text */ ``` ### Portfolio Data Pattern Store projects as JSON files in `content/portfolio/`: ```json { "title": "Project Name", "client": "Client Name", "category": "video|photo|brand|events|marketing", "year": "2025", "description": "ES description", "approach": "How we tackled it", "results": "What was achieved" } ``` Portfolio pages use `generateStaticParams()` reading from the JSON directory for SSG. ### Minimum Page Set for Agencies - Home (showreel hero + featured work + client logos) - Portfolio (filterable grid with URL-based filter) - Portfolio/[slug] (case study with description, approach, results, gallery, "quiero algo similar" CTA) - Services (expandable service cards) - About (team + values + story) - Clients (logo wall + testimonials) - Blog + Blog/[slug] - Contact (form + WhatsApp + map) - FAQ - Legal pages ### i18n for Agencies (ES/EN) Many PY agencies serve international clients. Implement language toggle early: - Content in `content/es.json` and `content/en.json` - URL-based switching: `/en/portfolio`, `/en/contact` - Detect via `Accept-Language` header on first visit, persist in localStorage - Store as simple JSON key-value pairs, not next-intl (too heavy for small sites) ## Technical Pitfalls ### Service-at-Home Business Model"}
